attributes.description: AWS RDS dashboard for CloudWatch metrics collected by the OpenTelemetry Collector.

embeddableConfig.description:
Monitor AWS RDS database instances across your fleet in real time.
Filter by DB instance at the top. Spot saturated CPU, idle databases, and storage outliers in the summary table — click any database to drill down.
To get started, instrument your RDS instances with the AWS integration.

attributes.description: Navigation tile — clicking opens the Kibana Dashboards library searched for “AWS OTel” dashboards in a new browser tab, carrying the current panel time range to the destination.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
ROW value = "AWS-Managed-OTel" | KEEP value

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
ROW value = "AWS-Managed-OTel" | KEEP value

embeddableConfig.description: Navigation tile — clicking opens the Kibana Dashboards library searched for “AWS OTel” dashboards in a new browser tab, carrying the current panel time range to the destination.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.rds.otel-*
| WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != ""
| STATS b_cpu = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))),
    b_storage = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL))),
    b_conn = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL)))
  BY timestamp = TBUCKET(50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region
| EVAL state = CASE(
    b_cpu IS NULL AND b_storage IS NULL AND b_conn IS NULL, "Unknown",
    b_cpu > 80, "saturated",
    b_storage < 5368709120, "low_storage",
    "healthy")
| STATS total = COUNT(*),
    saturated = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "saturated",
    low_storage = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "low_storage",
    healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "healthy"
  BY timestamp
| SORT timestamp ASC

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.rds.otel-*
| WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != ""
| STATS b_cpu = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))),
    b_storage = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL))),
    b_conn = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL)))
  BY timestamp = TBUCKET(50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region
| EVAL state = CASE(
    b_cpu IS NULL AND b_storage IS NULL AND b_conn IS NULL, "Unknown",
    b_cpu > 80, "saturated",
    b_storage < 5368709120, "low_storage",
    "healthy")
| STATS total = COUNT(*),
    saturated = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "saturated",
    low_storage = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "low_storage",
    healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "healthy"
  BY timestamp
| SORT timestamp ASC

embeddableConfig.description: Count of RDS databases over time, classified per time bucket by health — saturated (CPU > 80%), low-storage (free storage < 5 GiB), idle (no connections), or healthy. A rising saturated series indicates spreading CPU pressure.
embeddableConfig.title: Database count over time

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.rds.otel-*
| WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != ""
| STATS cpu_cur = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))),
    conn_cur = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL))),
    storage_cur = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL))),
    last_seen = MAX(@timestamp)
  BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen)
| WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5
| EVAL state = CASE(
    cpu_cur IS NULL AND conn_cur IS NULL AND storage_cur IS NULL, "Unknown",
    cpu_cur > 80, "saturated",
    storage_cur < 5368709120, "low-storage",
    "healthy")
| STATS databases = COUNT(*) BY rds_state = state

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.rds.otel-*
| WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != ""
| STATS cpu_cur = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))),
    conn_cur = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL))),
    storage_cur = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL))),
    last_seen = MAX(@timestamp)
  BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen)
| WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5
| EVAL state = CASE(
    cpu_cur IS NULL AND conn_cur IS NULL AND storage_cur IS NULL, "Unknown",
    cpu_cur > 80, "saturated",
    storage_cur < 5368709120, "low-storage",
    "healthy")
| STATS databases = COUNT(*) BY rds_state = state

embeddableConfig.description: Share of RDS databases by latest state — saturated (CPU > 80%), low-storage (free storage < 5 GiB), idle (no connections), or healthy. Uses the most recent value per database within a 5-minute freshness window.
embeddableConfig.title: Databases by status

embeddableConfig.description: One hexagon per RDS database, ordered worst-first with CPU-saturated or low-storage databases first. Toggle Color by between Status, CPU utilization, or Free Storage; metric views support Min, Max, Avg, and Last over the dashboard time range. Click any hexagon to filter the dashboard or open metrics for that database.

savedVis.description: One hexagon per RDS database, ordered worst-first with CPU-saturated or low-storage databases first. Toggle Color by between Status, CPU utilization, or Free Storage; metric views support Min, Max, Avg, and Last over the dashboard time range. Click any hexagon to filter the dashboard or open metrics for that database.
savedVis.title: RDS databases — hexagon map

embeddableConfig.title: RDS databases — hexagon map (click an hex for actions)

attributes.description: Total number of RDS databases — distinct DB instance identifiers reporting metrics in the latest collected data for the selected time range.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.rds.otel-* | WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != "" AND stat IN ("Average", "Maximum") | STATS last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest) <= 5 | STATS total = COUNT(*) | KEEP total

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.rds.otel-* | WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != "" AND stat IN ("Average", "Maximum") | STATS last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest) <= 5 | STATS total = COUNT(*) | KEEP total

embeddableConfig.description: Total number of RDS databases — distinct DB instance identifiers reporting metrics in the latest collected data for the selected time range.

attributes.description: Number of low-storage RDS databases — those whose free storage dropped below 5 GiB, within a 5-minute freshness window. At risk of running out of disk. (Aurora instances are excluded — their storage auto-scales.)

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.rds.otel-* | WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != "" AND stat IN ("Maximum", "Minimum") | STATS cpu_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)), conn_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL)), storage_cur = MIN(CASE(stat == "Minimum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL)), last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5 | EVAL state = CASE(cpu_cur IS NULL AND conn_cur IS NULL AND storage_cur IS NULL, "Unknown", cpu_cur > 80, "saturated", storage_cur < 5368709120, "low-storage", "healthy") | STATS low_storage = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "low-storage" | KEEP low_storage

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.rds.otel-* | WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != "" AND stat IN ("Maximum", "Minimum") | STATS cpu_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)), conn_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL)), storage_cur = MIN(CASE(stat == "Minimum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL)), last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5 | EVAL state = CASE(cpu_cur IS NULL AND conn_cur IS NULL AND storage_cur IS NULL, "Unknown", cpu_cur > 80, "saturated", storage_cur < 5368709120, "low-storage", "healthy") | STATS low_storage = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "low-storage" | KEEP low_storage

embeddableConfig.description: Number of low-storage RDS databases — those whose free storage dropped below 5 GiB, within a 5-minute freshness window. At risk of running out of disk. (Aurora instances are excluded — their storage auto-scales.)

| WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != ""

embeddableConfig.description: Number of saturated RDS databases — those whose latest CPU utilization exceeds 80%, within a 5-minute freshness window. Indicates CPU-bound databases that may slow queries.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.rds.otel-*
| WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != ""
| STATS
    c_cpu = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))) / 100.0,
    c_conn = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL))),
    c_mem = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeableMemory`, NULL))),
    c_storage = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL))),
    c_read_iops = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/ReadIOPS`, NULL))),
    c_write_iops = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/WriteIOPS`, NULL))),
    c_read_lat = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/ReadLatency`, NULL))) * 1000,
    c_write_lat = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/WriteLatency`, NULL))) * 1000,
    state_cpu = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))),
    state_storage = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL))),
    last_ms = MAX(TO_LONG(@timestamp))
  BY DBInstanceIdentifier, c_account = cloud.account.id, c_region = cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_ms = MAX(last_ms)
| WHERE (latest_ms - last_ms) <= 600000
| EVAL
    c_state_sort = CASE(
      state_cpu IS NULL AND state_storage IS NULL, 4,
      state_cpu > 80, 1,
      state_storage < 5368709120, 2,
      3),
    c_state = CASE(
      state_cpu IS NULL AND state_storage IS NULL, "Unknown",
      state_cpu > 80, "saturated",
      state_storage < 5368709120, "low-storage",
      "healthy")
| SORT c_state_sort ASC

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.rds.otel-*
| WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != ""
| STATS
    c_cpu = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))) / 100.0,
    c_conn = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL))),
    c_mem = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeableMemory`, NULL))),
    c_storage = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL))),
    c_read_iops = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/ReadIOPS`, NULL))),
    c_write_iops = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/WriteIOPS`, NULL))),
    c_read_lat = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/ReadLatency`, NULL))) * 1000,
    c_write_lat = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/WriteLatency`, NULL))) * 1000,
    state_cpu = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))),
    state_storage = AVG(avg_over_time(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL))),
    last_ms = MAX(TO_LONG(@timestamp))
  BY DBInstanceIdentifier, c_account = cloud.account.id, c_region = cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_ms = MAX(last_ms)
| WHERE (latest_ms - last_ms) <= 600000
| EVAL
    c_state_sort = CASE(
      state_cpu IS NULL AND state_storage IS NULL, 4,
      state_cpu > 80, 1,
      state_storage < 5368709120, 2,
      3),
    c_state = CASE(
      state_cpu IS NULL AND state_storage IS NULL, "Unknown",
      state_cpu > 80, "saturated",
      state_storage < 5368709120, "low-storage",
      "healthy")
| SORT c_state_sort ASC

embeddableConfig.description: All RDS databases, ranked worst-first by state. Click a database to drill into its metrics. State reflects the latest values within a 5-minute freshness window. Avg CPU, read/write latency, and IOPS are averaged over the selected time range; free memory, free storage, and connections are current (last) values.
embeddableConfig.title: Databases

attributes.description: Highest concurrent connections observed on any single database — peak over the selected time range. Approaching an instance's connection limit can reject new clients.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.rds.otel-* | WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != "" | WHERE stat IN ("Maximum", "Minimum") | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS cpu_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)), conn_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL)), storage_cur = MIN(CASE(stat == "Minimum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL)) BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region | EVAL state = CASE(cpu_cur IS NULL AND conn_cur IS NULL AND storage_cur IS NULL, "Unknown", cpu_cur > 80, "saturated", storage_cur < 5368709120, "low-storage", "healthy") | STATS total_conn = SUM(conn_cur) | KEEP total_conn

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.rds.otel-* | WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != "" | WHERE stat IN ("Maximum", "Minimum") | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS cpu_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)), conn_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL)), storage_cur = MIN(CASE(stat == "Minimum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL)) BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region | EVAL state = CASE(cpu_cur IS NULL AND conn_cur IS NULL AND storage_cur IS NULL, "Unknown", cpu_cur > 80, "saturated", storage_cur < 5368709120, "low-storage", "healthy") | STATS total_conn = SUM(conn_cur) | KEEP total_conn

embeddableConfig.description: Highest concurrent connections observed on any single database — peak over the selected time range. Approaching an instance's connection limit can reject new clients.

attributes.description: Total free storage across all non-Aurora databases — the sum of each database's lowest observed free storage (bytes) over the selected time range. Falling values indicate cluster-wide disk pressure.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.rds.otel-* | WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != "" | WHERE stat IN ("Maximum", "Minimum") | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS cpu_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)), conn_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL)), storage_cur = MIN(CASE(stat == "Minimum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL)) BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region | EVAL state = CASE(cpu_cur IS NULL AND conn_cur IS NULL AND storage_cur IS NULL, "Unknown", cpu_cur > 80, "saturated", storage_cur < 5368709120, "low-storage", "healthy") | STATS total_storage = SUM(storage_cur) | KEEP total_storage

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.rds.otel-* | WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != "" | WHERE stat IN ("Maximum", "Minimum") | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS cpu_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)), conn_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL)), storage_cur = MIN(CASE(stat == "Minimum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL)) BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region | EVAL state = CASE(cpu_cur IS NULL AND conn_cur IS NULL AND storage_cur IS NULL, "Unknown", cpu_cur > 80, "saturated", storage_cur < 5368709120, "low-storage", "healthy") | STATS total_storage = SUM(storage_cur) | KEEP total_storage

embeddableConfig.description: Total free storage across all non-Aurora databases — the sum of each database's lowest observed free storage (bytes) over the selected time range. Falling values indicate cluster-wide disk pressure.

attributes.description: Top 10 RDS databases by peak CPU utilization (%) over the selected time range. Reference line at 80% marks the high-CPU threshold; bars crossing it indicate CPU-bound databases.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.rds.otel-* | WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != "" | WHERE stat == "Maximum" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization` IS NOT NULL | STATS peak_cpu = MAX(max_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`)) / 100.0 BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region | SORT peak_cpu DESC | LIMIT 10

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.rds.otel-* | WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != "" | WHERE stat == "Maximum" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization` IS NOT NULL | STATS peak_cpu = MAX(max_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`)) / 100.0 BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region | SORT peak_cpu DESC | LIMIT 10

attributes.description: Top 10 RDS databases by peak read IOPS over the selected time range. High values flag read-heavy databases that may saturate storage throughput.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.rds.otel-* | WHERE DBInstanceIdentifier IS NOT NULL AND DBInstanceIdentifier != "" | WHERE stat == "Maximum" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/ReadIOPS` IS NOT NULL | STATS peak_read_iops = MAX(max_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/ReadIOPS`)) BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region | SORT peak_read_iops DESC | LIMIT 10
